person.name: Mircea Mihalea
person.bio: Mircea is a Cloud Solution Architect and platform engineering advocate, specializing in Kubernetes and cloud-native technologies. He designs and delivers large-scale, secure cloud architectures, with deep expertise in networking, infrastructure as code, and enterprise platform design. He leads cloud and DevOps competency initiatives, shaping standards, guiding architectural decisions, and enabling teams to adopt scalable platform engineering practices. As a community leader, he mentors engineers, leads technical knowledge-sharing, and supports the growth of the next generation of cloud professionals.

person.name: Alexandr Gumeniuc
person.bio: I'm a passionate cloud and automation engineer, DevOps enthusiast focused on maximizing automation through code. I started working with Kubernetes back in 2020 and absolutely loved it. My working stack is not limited to Kubernetes, other CNCF projects that I am using are: Helm, Argo, Kyverno. I prefer spending free time cycling, hiking or doing other outdoor activities.

person.name: Anh Quach
person.bio: I am currently working as a Senior DevOps Engineer with a strong focus on cloud-native technologies and CNCF tools such as Kubernetes, Prometheus, and Helm. With a passion for building scalable and resilient infrastructure, I also have hands-on experience in some specific areas, e.g., container orchestration like Kubernetes, microservices, CI/CD pipelines. I will continuously learn new cloud-native technology, and be deeply committed to continuously expanding my knowledge and exploring new advancements within the CNCF landscape.

person.name: Bradley Andersen
person.bio: Bradley is ex [Gitlab | Upbound | Capital One], a reformed [Perl hacker | TAM], a bridge builder and a glue guy. He enjoys working with others to solve complex problems, and unifying and growing people and processes.

person.name: Naveen Chandu
person.bio: I am a Site Reliability Engineer currently working with the Microsoft ADX team. With over 5 years of hands-on experience, I am a seasoned Kubernetes specialist and an avid IT and cloud enthusiast. My journey in the tech world began with Kubernetes, and it has been a thrilling adventure ever since. In my previous role as an AKS Engineer at Microsoft, I honed my skills in troubleshooting and resolving issues related to Kubernetes, ARO, ACI, ACR, and associated technologies such as Helm, Terraform, and Istio. My passion for cloud technologies drives me to continuously explore and engage with various CNCF tools.  Looking forward to connecting, collaborating, and contributing to this vibrant community!

person.name: Oliwer Borysewicz
person.bio: I am a Google Cloud Engineer passionate about Kubernetes and cloud automation. In my current role at Devoteam, I help clients adopt SRE practices, automate Terraform IaC and Cloud Build CI/CD pipelines, and address complex challenges in GKE, DevOps, DevSecOps, and MLOps. My fascination with Kubernetes extends beyond containerized applications – I'm excited about its potential for automating Data and Machine Learning pipelines like Cloud Composer or Kubeflow. Highly motivated to continuously expand my Kubernetes, DevSecOps & AI/ML knowledge, I believe my skills will be a valuable asset to the Kubestronaut community.

person.name: Patryk Chmarzynski
person.bio: I am a Platform Engineer who began my career working with physical servers and infrastructure before transitioning to virtualisation, containers, and GitOps. Over the years, I have worked with a wide range of technologies across both the public and private sectors. I enjoy building reliable platforms and working on challenging projects.

person.name: Ihor Dvoretskyi
person.bio:
Ihor is a Senior Developer Advocate at Cloud Native Computing Foundation, focused on Kubernetes-related efforts in the open source community.

He is a Product Manager for Kubernetes, co-leading the Product Management Special Interest Group, focused on enhancing Kubernetes as an open source product. Besides that, he is participating in Kubernetes release process as a features lead for multiple Kubernetes releases.

A Linux user since 2005 with a deep engineering background, Ihor has been responsible for projects tightly bound to the cloud computing space, containerized workloads and Linux systems.

His recent speaking appearances include talks at Kubernetes Leadership and Contributors Summits, OpenStack Summits NA and APAC, OpenSource Summit EU, ContainerDays EU, Google DevFests and other industry-leading events.
